instrument_full_name: Attachment Questionnaire for Children (AQC; Muris et al.)
ordered_questions: I find it relatively easy to get close to other children and feel comfortable depending on them and having them depend on me. I don't worry about being alone or about other children not accepting me. ; I am somewhat uncomfortable being close to other children; I find it difficult to trust them completely, difficult to allow myself to depend on them. I am nervous when any child gets too close to me, and often other children want me to be more intimate than I feel comfortable being. ; I find that other children are reluctant to get as close as I would like. I often worry that my friends don't really like me or won't want to stay my friend. I want to be very close to other children, and this sometimes scares them away.
notes: Muris, Mayer & Meesters (2000); single-item 3-category child self-report adapted from Hazan & Shaver's (1987) adult three-category measure, reworded for children (Secure / Avoidant / Ambivalent). Distributed freely for research (NCTSN listing; Hazan & Shaver basis is in the freely distributed Fetzer/UC Davis self-report compendium). Item wording above is the standard child-adapted three-paragraph forced-choice; verify exact child wording against the original article.

instrument_full_name: Barratt Impulsiveness Scale, version 11 (BIS-11)
ordered_questions: I plan tasks carefully. ; I do things without thinking. ; I make-up my mind quickly. ; I am happy-go-lucky. ; I don't "pay attention." ; I have "racing" thoughts. ; I plan trips well ahead of time. ; I am self controlled. ; I concentrate easily. ; I save regularly. ; I "squirm" at plays or lectures. ; I am a careful thinker. ; I plan for job security. ; I say things without thinking. ; I like to think about complex problems. ; I change jobs. ; I act "on impulse." ; I get easily bored when solving thought problems. ; I act on the spur of the moment. ; I am a steady thinker. ; I change residences. ; I buy things on impulse. ; I can only think about one thing at a time. ; I change hobbies. ; I spend or charge more than I earn. ; I often have extraneous thoughts when thinking. ; I am more interested in the present than the future. ; I am restless at the theater or lectures. ; I like puzzles. ; I am future oriented.
notes: Patton, Stanford & Barratt (1995). Freely available for research/clinical use with attribution; widely disseminated by the authors (M. Stanford, Baylor). 30 items, 4-point scale (Rarely/Never to Almost Always/Always). Items verified from official English version.

rdoc_label: Bartholomew and Shaver Adult Attachment Scale
instrument_full_name: Relationship Questionnaire (RQ; Bartholomew & Horowitz)
ordered_questions: It is easy for me to become emotionally close to others. I am comfortable depending on them and having them depend on me. I don't worry about being alone or having others not accept me. ; I am uncomfortable getting close to others. I want emotionally close relationships, but I find it difficult to trust others completely, or to depend on them. I worry that I will be hurt if I allow myself to become too close to others. ; I want to be completely emotionally intimate with others, but I often find that others are reluctant to get as close as I would like. I am uncomfortable being without close relationships, but I sometimes worry that others don't value me as much as I value them. ; I am comfortable without close emotional relationships. It is very important to me to feel independent and self-sufficient, and I prefer not to depend on others or have others depend on me.
notes: Bartholomew & Horowitz (1991), JPSP 61, 226-244. Four-paragraph forced-choice + 7-point rating of each style (A=Secure, B=Fearful, C=Preoccupied, D=Dismissing). Freely distributed for research via the Fetzer/UC Davis 'Self Report Measures for Love and Compassion Research' compendium and Shaver/UC Davis measures site. Items captured verbatim in standard order A-D.

rdoc_label: Drive subscale of the Behavioral Activation Scale
instrument_full_name: BAS Drive subscale of the Carver & White BIS/BAS Scales
ordered_questions: I go out of my way to get things I want. ; When I want something I usually go all-out to get it. ; If I see a chance to get something I want I move on it right away. ; When I go after something I use a "no holds barred" approach.
notes: Carver & White (1994), JPSP 67, 319-333. Full BIS/BAS scale and scoring freely distributed by author C. S. Carver (University of Miami) for research use. Drive subscale = items 3, 9, 12, 21 of the 24-item BIS/BAS. Response scale 1=very true for me to 4=very false for me; reverse-scored except items 2 and 22.

rdoc_label: Multidimensional Scale of Perceived Social Support
instrument_full_name: Multidimensional Scale of Perceived Social Support (MSPSS)
ordered_questions: There is a special person who is around when I am in need. ; There is a special person with whom I can share my joys and sorrows. ; My family really tries to help me. ; I get the emotional help and support I need from my family. ; I have a special person who is a real source of comfort to me. ; My friends really try to help me. ; I can count on my friends when things go wrong. ; I have friends with whom I can share my joys and sorrows. ; There is a special person in my life who cares about my feelings. ; My family is willing to help me make decisions. ; I can talk about my problems with my family. ; I can talk about my problems with my friends.
notes: Zimet, Dahlem, Zimet & Farley (1988). Explicitly free to use with citation of the original paper (per author's official site). 12 items, 7-point agree-disagree scale; subscales: Significant Other (1,2,5,10), Family (3,4,8,11), Friends (6,7,9,12).

rdoc_label: Positive and Negative Affect Schedule (state version)
instrument_full_name: Positive and Negative Affect Schedule - state version (PANAS; Watson, Clark & Tellegen)
ordered_questions: interested ; distressed ; excited ; upset ; strong ; guilty ; scared ; hostile ; enthusiastic ; proud ; irritable ; alert ; ashamed ; inspired ; nervous ; determined ; attentive ; jittery ; active ; afraid
notes: Watson, Clark & Tellegen (1988), JPSP 54, 1063-1070. 20 affect terms (10 Positive Affect, 10 Negative Affect) rated 1 (very slightly/not at all) to 5 (extremely). State version uses 'moment' or 'today' time instructions. APA holds journal copyright but the scale is widely distributed and free for research/clinical (non-commercial) use; items reproduced in the article appendix. Items captured in the order printed in the original appendix.

rdoc_label: Adult Temperament Questionnaire/Children's Behavioral Questionnaire Effortful Control
instrument_full_name: Adult Temperament Questionnaire / Children's Behavior Questionnaire - Effortful Control (Rothbart; Evans & Rothbart)
notes: Rothbart temperament questionnaires (Evans & Rothbart, 2007 for ATQ; Rothbart et al. for CBQ). Freely available for research (permission not required) via Bowdoin/Rothbart lab. ATQ short-form Effortful Control = 19 items across Activation Control, Attentional Control, Inhibitory Control subscales (7-point scale). A verified verbatim ordered item list could not be confirmed from an open source in this pass, so items left blank; obtain official item list from research.bowdoin.edu. n_items reflects ATQ short-form Effortful Control.

instrument_full_name: Aberrant Behavior Checklist (ABC; Aman & Singh)
notes: Aman & Singh (1986). 58 items across 5 subscales (Irritability 15, Lethargy/Social Withdrawal 16, Stereotypic Behavior 7, Hyperactivity/Noncompliance 16, Inappropriate Speech 4). Copyright Slosson Educational Publications Inc. Obtain from publisher. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Activation-Deactivation Adjective Check List (AD ACL; Thayer, 1978/1986)
notes: Self-report adjective checklist of momentary arousal (energetic arousal, tense arousal and their deactivation poles); short form ~20 adjectives. Copyright held by R. E. Thayer; not public domain. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: ADHD Rating Scale (DuPaul, Power, Anastopoulos & Reid)
notes: DuPaul, Power, Anastopoulos & Reid. 18 items mapped to DSM ADHD criteria (ADHD-IV and ADHD-5 versions). Copyright Guilford Press; purchasers receive limited photocopy license for own clinical use only. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Adult Attachment Interview (AAI; George, Kaplan & Main)
notes: George, Kaplan & Main (1985, unpublished manuscript, UC Berkeley). Semi-structured ~1 hour interview (approx. 20 questions/probes about childhood attachment) coded by trained raters; NOT a self-report item questionnaire. Protocol author-controlled/unpublished; coding requires certified training. Items not captured (interview, not a self-report scale).

rdoc_label: American Society of Addiction Medicine criteria
instrument_full_name: The ASAM Criteria (American Society of Addiction Medicine)
notes: Multidimensional assessment across 6 dimensions for substance use treatment placement. Copyright 2015 American Society of Addiction Medicine; reproduction prohibited without written permission (ASAM also explicitly prohibits input into AI systems). Obtain via ASAM licensing. Items withheld; not a conventional self-report item scale.

instrument_full_name: Anxiety Sensitivity Index (ASI)
notes: Reiss, Peterson, Gursky & McNally (1986); items written and copyrighted by Steven Reiss (1984). 16 items, 5-point scale (0-4). Distributed by IDS Publishing Corporation. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Balanced Emotional Empathy Scale (BEES; Mehrabian, 1996/1997)
notes: Copyrighted by Albert Mehrabian; manual obtained directly from the author. 30 items, 9-point Likert. Items not reproduced.

rdoc_label: Behavior Rating Inventory of Executive Function
instrument_full_name: Behavior Rating Inventory of Executive Function (BRIEF; Gioia et al.)
notes: Published by PAR Inc. (Lutz, FL). Original BRIEF parent/teacher forms 86 items; BRIEF2 and BRIEF-A (adult, 75 items) also exist. Copyright PAR Inc. Purchase from publisher. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Childhood Trauma Questionnaire - Short Form (CTQ-SF)
notes: Bernstein & Fink (1998); CTQ-SF Bernstein et al. (2003). 28 items (25 clinical + 3 minimization/denial), 5 subscales, 5-point Likert. Published and distributed by Pearson (Pearson Assessments / NCS Pearson). Purchase/license from Pearson; items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Cognitive Assessment Interview (CAI; Ventura et al.)
notes: Ventura et al. (2010), Schizophrenia Research. 10-item interview-based co-primary cognition measure for schizophrenia (interviewer-rated, with patient and informant input); 15-30 min. Not a conventional self-report item list. Copyright authors/publisher. Obtain from authors/article. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Cognitive Failures Questionnaire (CFQ; Broadbent et al.)
notes: Broadbent, Cooper, FitzGerald & Parkes (1982), British Journal of Clinical Psychology 21, 1-16. 25 items rated on 5-point frequency scale over past 6 months. Published by the British Psychological Society; reproduction of items generally requires BPS/journal permission (copyright status reported inconsistently). Items withheld to be safe. Obtain from original article/BPS.

instrument_full_name: Conners Rating Scales - Hyperactivity/Impulsivity (Conners; MHS)
notes: Conners Rating Scales (C. Keith Conners). Hyperactivity/Impulsivity content within Conners 3 / Conners 4 (parent, teacher, self-report forms). Copyright Multi-Health Systems (MHS) Inc. Purchase from publisher. Items withheld.

rdoc_label: Consummatory subscale of Temporal Experience of Pleasure Scale
instrument_full_name: Temporal Experience of Pleasure Scale - Consummatory subscale (TEPS; Gard et al.)
notes: Gard, Gard, Kring & John (2006), Journal of Research in Personality 40, 1086-1102. Consummatory subscale = 8 items of the 18-item TEPS; 6-point Likert. Published in a copyrighted Elsevier journal with no open license; verified ordered item list not openly available. Obtain from authors/original article. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Eating Expectancy Inventory (EEI; Hohlstein, Smith & Atlas)
notes: Hohlstein, Smith & Atlas (1998), Psychological Assessment 10, 49-58. 34 items, 7-point Likert; five subscales (Negative Affect management, Reward, Out of Control, Boredom, Cognitive Competence). APA-journal copyrighted. Obtain from APA PsycTests / original article. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Fear Survey Schedule (FSS; Wolpe & Lang)
notes: Wolpe & Lang Fear Survey Schedule (FSS-III, 108 items; manual published by Educational and Industrial Testing Service, San Diego). Items rated 0 (not at all) to 4 (very much). Commercial/manual-based instrument; copyright holder historically difficult to locate. Obtain via EdITS/original manual. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Insomnia Severity Index (ISI)
notes: Bastien, Vallieres & Morin (2001); originally Morin (1993). 7 items, 5-point (0-4) scale. Copyrighted by Charles M. Morin / Mapi Research Trust (eProvide). Obtain via Mapi/eProvide. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Intolerance of Uncertainty Scale (IUS)
notes: Freeston, Rheaume, Letarte, Dugas & Ladouceur (1994); original French, English version validated by Buhr & Dugas (2002). 27 items, 5-point Likert. Contact original authors for permission. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Levels of Emotional Awareness Scale (LEAS; Lane, Quinlan, Schwartz, Walker & Zeitlin, 1990)
notes: Performance-based measure: 20 evocative interpersonal scenarios scored 0-3 by trained raters using the LEAS manual/glossary. Not a brief Likert item set. Manual/scoring proprietary; obtain from authors.

instrument_full_name: Life Events and Difficulties Schedule (LEDS)
notes: Brown & Harris (1978). Investigator-based ~2-hour semi-structured interview rated against a 520-page manual with ~5000 case vignettes; requires formal LEDS training (King's College London). Not an item-based self-report; no reproducible item list. Obtain via LEDS training course.

instrument_full_name: Lille Apathy Rating Scale (LARS)
notes: Sockeel et al. (2006), J. Neurology, Neurosurgery & Psychiatry; original language French. Structured interview, 33 items in 9 domains, dichotomous scoring (clinician-administered rather than pure self-report). Published copyrighted article. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Morningness-Eveningness Questionnaire (MEQ)
notes: Horne & Ostberg (1976), International Journal of Chronobiology. 19 items. Copyright (c) 1976 Gordon and Breach, Science Publishers. Items not reproduced; self-assessment (MEQ-SA) version distributed via the Center for Environmental Therapeutics (cet.org).

instrument_full_name: Munich ChronoType Questionnaire (MCTQ)
notes: Roenneberg, Wirz-Justice & Merrow (2003); copyright (c) Till Roenneberg & Martha Merrow / co-workers, LMU Munich. Captures clock times (sleep/wake on work and free days) used to compute chronotype (MSFsc); ~17-19 items depending on module/version. Freely available for non-commercial research from the WeP/THeWeP repository with attribution, but author-copyrighted, so items not reproduced here.

rdoc_label: Other components of Attributional Styles Questionnaires
instrument_full_name: Attributional Style Questionnaire (ASQ; Peterson et al., 1982) - other (stability/globality/externality, positive vs negative event) components
notes: Companion entry to 151129; refers to the non-self causal dimensions (stable/global, externality) and positive/negative composites of the ASQ. Permission required from M. Seligman. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Profile of Mood States (POMS) - arousal/vigor-related subscale
notes: POMS (McNair, Lorr & Droppleman, 1971) published and copyrighted by Multi-Health Systems Inc. (MHS). Full POMS = 65 items across 6 subscales (tension, depression, anger, vigor-activity, fatigue, confusion); arousal indexed via the vigor-activity subscale (8 items). Purchase/license from MHS. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Risky Families Questionnaire (RFQ)
notes: Felitti et al. items adapted by Taylor and colleagues; theoretical basis Repetti, Taylor & Seeman (2002). 11 items, 4-point Likert, retrospective childhood family environment (ages 5-15). Registered in APA PsycTests (t03616-000). Items not reproduced.

rdoc_label: Rush Video Based Tic Rating Scale
instrument_full_name: Rush Video-Based Tic Rating Scale
notes: Goetz et al. (original 1987; modified scoring Goetz et al., 1999), Movement Disorders. Clinician video-based rating across 5 tic-disability domains (body areas, motor tic frequency, phonic tic frequency, motor tic severity, phonic tic severity), each 0-4 - not a self-report. Permission required for use. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Scale for the Assessment of Negative Symptoms (SANS) / Scale for the Assessment of Positive Symptoms (SAPS) / Positive and Negative Syndrome Scale (PANSS)
notes: Three clinician-rated (not self-report) schizophrenia symptom scales. SANS (Andreasen, 1983) and SAPS (Andreasen, 1984) developed at University of Iowa; PANSS (Kay, Fiszbein & Opler, 1987) distributed by Multi-Health Systems (MHS). SANS=25 items, SAPS=34 items, PANSS=30 items. Obtain SANS/SAPS from N. Andreasen / University of Iowa; PANSS from MHS. Items not reproduced (copyrighted).

rdoc_label: Self Components of Attributional Styles Questionnaire
instrument_full_name: Attributional Style Questionnaire (ASQ; Peterson, Semmel, von Baeyer, Abramson, Metalsky & Seligman, 1982) - self/internality components
notes: ASQ: 12 hypothetical events, rated on internal/stable/global causal dimensions (60 ratings). 'Self components' = internality/self-attribution dimension. Permission required from M. Seligman (Penn Positive Psychology Center). Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Self-Assessment Manikin (SAM; Bradley & Lang, 1994)
notes: Nonverbal pictorial rating of affect on 3 dimensions (valence/pleasure, arousal, dominance). Published in J. Behav. Ther. Exp. Psychiatry; used with permission of Bradley & Lang (NIMH Center for Emotion and Attention, Univ. of Florida). Graphic figures not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Social Responsiveness Scale, Second Edition (SRS-2; Constantino, 2012)
notes: Proprietary instrument published by Western Psychological Services (WPS). 65 items. Requires purchase/license; items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Stress and Adversity Inventory for Adults (Adult STRAIN)
notes: Developed and copyrighted by George Slavich (UCLA). Online adaptive system assessing lifetime acute life events and chronic difficulties (counts/coding rather than a fixed public item list). Access requires authorization via the STRAIN setup process; unauthorized use not permitted. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Temporal Experience of Pleasure Scale - Anticipatory subscale (TEPS; Gard et al.)
notes: Gard, Gard, Kring & John (2006), Journal of Research in Personality 40, 1086-1102. 18-item TEPS = 10-item Anticipatory + 8-item Consummatory; 6-point Likert (1=very false to 6=very true). Published in a copyrighted Elsevier journal with no open license; complete verified ordered item list not openly available. Obtain from authors/original article. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Toronto Alexithymia Scale-20 (TAS-20; Bagby, Parker & Taylor, 1994)
notes: Proprietary; licensing required from Dr. Graeme J. Taylor (University of Toronto). 20 items, 5-point Likert, 3 subscales. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Yale Global Tic Severity Scale (YGTSS)
notes: Leckman et al. (1989), J. American Academy of Child & Adolescent Psychiatry. Clinician-rated (semi-structured interview + severity ratings across number, frequency, intensity, complexity, interference for motor and phonic tics, plus an impairment scale) - not a self-report item list. Items not reproduced.

rdoc_label: Yale-Brown Obsessive Compulsive Scale total score
instrument_full_name: Yale-Brown Obsessive Compulsive Scale (Y-BOCS; Goodman et al.)
notes: Goodman et al. (1989). 10-item clinician-administered OCD severity scale; total score 0-40 (Obsession items 1-5 and Compulsion items 6-10, each 0-4). Copyright Wayne K. Goodman et al. Obtain from authors / licensing. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Youth Life Stress Interview (YLSI)
notes: Rudolph & Flynn (2007), adapted from the UCLA Child Episodic Life Stress Interview (Rudolph & Hammen, 1999). Semi-structured contextual-threat stress interview, not an item-based self-report. Materials available from the Hammen lab (UCLA). No reproducible item list.

instrument_full_name: Berkeley Expressivity Questionnaire (BEQ; Gross & John, 1995/1997)
notes: 16-item self-report measure of emotional expressivity (Negative Expressivity, Positive Expressivity, Impulse Strength), 7-point Likert. Developed by Gross & John. Widely used in research; copyright held by authors, no explicit public-domain release found. Items in original journal article.

instrument_full_name: Empathy Quotient (EQ; Baron-Cohen & Wheelwright, 2004)
notes: 60 items (40 scored empathy items + 20 filler), 4-point scale. Distributed for research by Cambridge Autism Research Centre; copyright held by author/University of Cambridge, not formally public domain. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Parental Bonding Instrument (PBI; Parker, Tupling & Brown, 1979)
notes: 25-item retrospective self-report of perceived parental Care and Overprotection, completed separately for mother and father, 4-point scale. Originally published in Br. J. Med. Psychol.; widely treated as free for research/clinical use by the author, but no explicit public-domain dedication. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Subjective Units of Distress Scale (SUDS; Wolpe & Lazarus)
ordered_questions: Right now, how would you rate your level of distress (anxiety/discomfort) on a scale from 0 to 100, where 0 = absolutely calm/no distress and 100 = the worst distress you have ever experienced or can imagine?
notes: Single-item subjective rating scale (0-100; sometimes 0-10) introduced by Wolpe (1969) / Wolpe & Lazarus (1966). Not a multi-item copyrighted questionnaire; a generic anchored rating method in the public domain. The wording is a standard reconstruction of the instruction; exact phrasing varies by user.

instrument_full_name: Attachment Style Interview (ASI; Bifulco et al., 2002)
notes: Semi-structured investigator-based interview (not a brief self-report item set). Coded by trained, licensed raters; training/materials proprietary (Lifespan Research Group / A. Bifulco). No item list applicable; obtain via training.

instrument_full_name: Bereavement / complicated-grief self-report scales (e.g., Inventory of Complicated Grief; Prigerson et al., 1995)
notes: Generic RDoC category for grief/bereavement measures; prototypical instrument is the Inventory of Complicated Grief (ICG), 19 items, 5-point scale. Published in Psychiatry Research; copyright held by authors. Items not reproduced; multiple alternative grief scales also fall under this label.

rdoc_label: Diary-based measures of daily regularity/ rhythmicity (e.g., Social Rhythm Metric)
instrument_full_name: Social Rhythm Metric (SRM; Monk, Flaherty, Frank, Hoskinson & Kupfer, 1990)
notes: Diary instrument tracking timing of daily activities to derive a regularity (rhythmicity) index; full (17-item) and brief (5-item) versions exist. Copyright held by authors (Univ. of Pittsburgh); obtain from original publication. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Fear of Negative Evaluation Scale (FNE)
notes: Watson & Friend (1969). Original FNE = 30 true/false items; brief version (BFNE; Leary, 1983) = 12 items. Published in J. Consulting and Clinical Psychology (copyrighted). Contact authors/publisher; items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Frustrative Nonreward Responsiveness (FNR) subscale (extension of the BIS/BAS scales)
notes: 5-item subscale measuring reduced approach motivation following nonreward; extension of the Carver & White BIS/BAS scales, published in Personality and Individual Differences (2009). 4-point response (Very true to Very false). NIMH RDoC unit page: https://www.nimh.nih.gov/research/research-funded-by-nimh/rdoc/units/self-reports/153843. Items not reproduced (copyrighted journal).

rdoc_label: Generalized reward and punishment expectancy scale
instrument_full_name: Generalized Reward and Punishment Expectancy Scales (GRAPES; Ball & Zuckerman)
notes: Ball & Zuckerman (1990), 'The psychometric properties of a new measure of generalized expectancies of reward and punishment.' Two factors: Reward Expectancy and Punishment Expectancy. Published in copyrighted journal; full item list not openly available. Obtain from original article/authors. Items withheld.

instrument_full_name: Hyperacusis (category; e.g., Hyperacusis Questionnaire, Khalfa et al.)
notes: RDoC category. The most common named self-report instrument is the Hyperacusis Questionnaire (Khalfa et al., 2002; 14 items, 3 subscales: attentional, social, emotional), published in a copyrighted journal (Audiology & Neuro-Otology). Obtain from original article. Items withheld.

rdoc_label: Inventory of Parent and Peer Attachment Scale
instrument_full_name: Inventory of Parent and Peer Attachment (IPPA; Armsden & Greenberg)
notes: Armsden & Greenberg (1987/1989, University of Washington). Measures Trust, Communication, Alienation toward parents and peers; original 28 items per attachment figure (IPPA), revised IPPA-R 25 items x 3 figures (~75). Distributed by author (armsden.com) but reproduction is author-controlled (not an explicit open license). Obtain from author. Items withheld.

rdoc_label: Phase, diurnal preference, chronotype (e.g., Horne-Ostberg, CTQ)
instrument_full_name: Chronotype / diurnal preference measures (e.g., Horne-Ostberg Morningness-Eveningness Questionnaire; Composite/Munich chronotype questionnaires)
notes: RDoC category covering several chronotype instruments. Prototype is the Horne & Ostberg (1976) Morningness-Eveningness Questionnaire (MEQ; 19 items). MEQ items are reproduced in some sources but original copyright held by authors/journal. Items not reproduced; obtain from original publications.

instrument_full_name: Private Self-Consciousness subscale of the Self-Consciousness Scale (Fenigstein, Scheier & Buss, 1975)
notes: Private Self-Consciousness is one subscale (10 items) of the 23-item Self-Consciousness Scale published in an APA journal (J. Pers. Soc. Psychol.). Copyright held by APA/authors. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Questionnaire of Daily Frustrations (QDF)
notes: Self-report developed to discriminate punishment vs. nonreward via depressive and aggressive reactions; male version translated from an original German version (validated in male alcoholics). Published in a peer-reviewed journal (copyrighted). NIMH RDoC unit page: https://www.nimh.nih.gov/research/research-funded-by-nimh/rdoc/units/self-reports/153844. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Self-Monitoring Scale (Snyder, 1974)
notes: 25-item true/false scale published in an APA journal (J. Pers. Soc. Psychol., 1974); copyright APA/Snyder. (Revised 18-item version Snyder & Gangestad 1986 also exists.) Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Sleep quality/quantity self-report measures (e.g., Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index)
notes: Generic RDoC category; prototypical instrument is the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI; Buysse et al., 1989), 19 self-rated items. PSQI copyright held by University of Pittsburgh (free for non-commercial research under license). Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Traumatic Events Screening Inventory (TESI)
notes: Ford, Racusin, Rogers, Ellis, Schiffman, Ribbe et al. Child (TESI-C/CRF) and parent (TESI-PRR) report versions; ~18+ items/probes about potentially traumatic events. Available free of charge from authors (J. Ford; C. Ghosh-Ippen); a TESI-C version is hosted by the U.S. VA National Center for PTSD. Treated as copyrighted/author-controlled; items not reproduced here.

instrument_full_name: Affective forecasting (paradigm/construct; not a single instrument)
notes: Not a single standardized self-report instrument. Affective forecasting (Wilson & Gilbert, 2003/2005) is a research paradigm in which participants predict the valence, specific emotions, intensity, and duration of future affective states; operationalized with ad hoc forecast items plus standard mood measures (e.g., PANAS, SWLS). No canonical item set; items not applicable.

instrument_full_name: Auditory hallucinations (category; e.g., AHRS / PSYRATS auditory hallucinations subscale)
notes: RDoC category, not a single self-report scale. Common measures: Auditory Hallucinations Rating Scale (AHRS; Hoffman), Auditory Vocal Hallucination Rating Scale (AVHRS), and PSYRATS auditory hallucinations subscale - mostly clinician/interviewer-rated. Copyright varies by instrument. Items not captured (category).

instrument_full_name: Experiences in Close Relationships scale (ECR; Brennan, Clark & Shaver, 1998)
notes: 36-item self-report of adult romantic attachment (Anxiety, Avoidance), 7-point Likert. Freely distributed for research (e.g., by R. C. Fraley); copyright held by authors, not formally public domain. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Face dimensional rating scales (paradigm; valence/arousal ratings of faces, e.g., via SAM)
notes: Not a single named instrument. A paradigm in which participants rate facial stimuli on emotional dimensions (typically valence, arousal, sometimes dominance), often using the Self-Assessment Manikin (Bradley & Lang, 1994) 9-point scales or Likert dimensional ratings. No fixed item list; depends on stimulus set. Items not applicable (rating paradigm).

instrument_full_name: Measures of repetitive behaviors (category; e.g., RBS-R / RBQ-2A)
notes: RDoC category, not a single instrument. Common measures: Repetitive Behavior Scale-Revised (RBS-R; Bodfish et al., 43 items, 6 subscales) and Repetitive Behaviours Questionnaire-2/RBQ-2A (self-report, ~20 items, Newcastle University). RBQ-2 freely available for research from Newcastle; RBS-R copyright varies. Items not captured (category).

instrument_full_name: Perceptual Aberration Scale (PAS; Chapman, Chapman & Raulin, 1978; a Wisconsin Schizotypy Scale)
notes: 35-item true/false self-report of body-image/perceptual distortions. Wisconsin Schizotypy Scales are distributed by the Chapman/Kwapil lab for research use but are not formally public domain; copyright held by authors. Items not reproduced.

rdoc_label: Perceptual anomalies of schizophrenia and depression
instrument_full_name: Perceptual anomalies of schizophrenia and depression (category; e.g., Chapman Perceptual Aberration Scale)
notes: RDoC category, not a single instrument. Related self-report measures include the Chapman Perceptual Aberration Scale (35 true/false items) and other psychosis-proneness scales. Copyright varies; Chapman scales distributed by the authors for research. Items not captured (category).

rdoc_label: Perspective Taking and Empathic Concern subscales of the Interpersonal Reactivity Index
instrument_full_name: Interpersonal Reactivity Index (IRI; Davis, 1980/1983) - Perspective Taking & Empathic Concern subscales
notes: IRI is a 28-item measure with four 7-item subscales; Perspective Taking + Empathic Concern = 14 items, 5-point scale. Freely distributed by M. Davis (Eckerd College) for research; copyright held by author, not formally public domain. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Attachment Q-Sort / Q-Set (AQS; Waters & Deane, 1985)
notes: Q-sort methodology: 90-100 behavioral descriptors sorted into 9 piles by observer or caregiver to index secure-base behavior (ages ~12-48 mo). Distributed by E. Waters (Stony Brook attachment lab) for research; copyright held by authors. Card descriptors not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Self-report of craving (category; multiple instruments)
notes: RDoC category, not a single named instrument. Common measures: Penn Alcohol Craving Scale, Alcohol Urge Questionnaire (8 items), Questionnaire on Smoking Urges, Brief Substance Craving Scale (16 items, NIDA), single-item 0-100 craving VAS. Copyright varies by instrument; obtain from respective sources. Items not captured (category).

instrument_full_name: Revised Social Anhedonia Scale (RSAS; Eckblad, Chapman, Chapman & Mishlove, 1982)
notes: 40-item true/false self-report. A Wisconsin (Chapman) psychosis-proneness scale, distributed by the Chapman/Kwapil lab for research use; copyright held by authors, not formally public domain. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Alertness (RDoC construct category; not a single named instrument)
notes: Generic RDoC measurement category. Alertness is commonly assessed via visual-analogue/Likert ratings or the Karolinska Sleepiness Scale; no single canonical self-report named here.

instrument_full_name: Arousal ratings (generic self-report ratings of subjective arousal; not a single named published instrument)
notes: Generic RDoC measure category for subjective arousal ratings (commonly via Self-Assessment Manikin or Likert arousal scales). Not a single named instrument; depends on protocol.

instrument_full_name: Dream reports (RDoC construct category; not a single named instrument)
notes: Generic RDoC measurement category. Dream reports are collected via free-recall dream logs/diaries; no standardized item-based self-report instrument is named.

instrument_full_name: Fatigue (RDoC construct category; not a single named instrument)
notes: Generic RDoC measurement category. Often measured via Fatigue Severity Scale, PROMIS Fatigue, or POMS fatigue subscale; no single canonical instrument specified.

instrument_full_name: Sleep timing (RDoC construct category; not a single named instrument)
notes: Generic RDoC construct measurement category rather than a specific copyrighted instrument. Sleep timing is typically captured via sleep diaries or chronotype questionnaires (e.g., MCTQ, MEQ). No single canonical instrument to attribute.

instrument_full_name: Sleep-modulated symptoms (RDoC construct category; not a single named instrument)
notes: Generic RDoC measurement category referring to symptoms that vary with sleep; no single canonical self-report instrument is named.

instrument_full_name: Sleepiness (RDoC construct category; not a single named instrument)
notes: Generic RDoC measurement category. Commonly assessed with the Epworth Sleepiness Scale, Stanford Sleepiness Scale, or Karolinska Sleepiness Scale; no single canonical instrument specified.

instrument_full_name: Sleepiness/alertness/mood self-report measures (e.g., Karolinska Sleepiness Scale, Stanford Sleepiness Scale, visual-analog alertness/mood scales)
notes: Generic RDoC category, not a single named instrument. Encompasses single-item/short sleepiness-alertness scales (KSS 1-9; SSS 1-7) plus mood/well-being VAS. Source/copyright varies by instrument. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Social/interpersonal subscales of depression inventories (e.g., interpersonal items of the IDAS, or social-withdrawal facets of standard depression scales)
notes: Generic RDoC category, not a single named instrument. Refers to social/interpersonal-functioning subscales drawn from broader copyrighted/validated depression measures. Source and copyright depend on the parent instrument selected. Items not reproduced.

instrument_full_name: Specific sleep symptoms (RDoC construct category; not a single named instrument)
notes: Generic RDoC measurement category. Symptom-specific sleep measures (e.g., insomnia, apnea, restless legs scales) vary; no single canonical instrument specified.
